name: Steel Browser
overview: Steel is an open-source browser API that specializes in long-running, persistent cloud browser sessions for AI applications.
details: Many automated web tasks fail because websites log the user out or require complex two-factor authentication (2FA) that an AI cannot easily bypass on its own. Steel solves this by allowing users to maintain persistent browser states, saving authentication cookies and session data so the AI can pick up exactly where it left off yesterday.

When connected to OpenClaw, Steel acts as the agent's dedicated remote workstation. An OpenClaw agent can launch a session, have the human user manually solve a 2FA prompt via a live viewer URL, and then let the AI take over to run tasks for the next 24 hours.

This persistent session management is critical for OpenClaw deployments that manage corporate social media accounts, interact with bank portals, or handle any continuous workflow where losing the logged-in state would break the automation.
